title: Excel / Google Sheets Data Manager (Real Estate Data Systems)
description:
JOB OVERVIEW



We are looking for a highly skilled Excel / Google Sheets Data Manager to build and manage a real estate data system.



This role involves working with large datasets, cleaning data, and creating a system that can combine multiple lists into one organized master sheet.



If you are NOT experienced with advanced Excel/Sheets, do NOT apply.



WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

Cleaning and organizing raw data

Standardizing property addresses

Combining (stacking) multiple datasets

Removing duplicates

Tagging properties based on list source

Ranking properties based on motivation

Preparing data for skip tracing

REQUIRED SKILLS

Advanced Excel / Google Sheets

Strong data organization skills

Experience with formulas (VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, FILTER, etc.)

Ability to build automated systems

Attention to detail

?? MANDATORY TEST TASK (REQUIRED TO APPLY)



To be considered, you MUST complete the task below and submit it with your application.



OBJECTIVE:



Create a spreadsheet system that can:



Clean and organize raw data

Combine (stack) multiple lists

Prevent duplicate properties

Tag properties based on list source

Rank properties based on motivation

SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS



Your system must include:



Master Sheet (Main Database):



Property Address

City

State

ZIP Code

Owner Name (optional)

Phone Number (optional)

List Source Tags

Motivation Score

Lead Status (dropdown: New, Contacted, Follow-Up, Dead, Deal)

Notes

DATA RULES

Each property must appear ONLY ONCE

If a property appears in multiple lists:

DO NOT duplicate it

Add multiple tags (example: “Pre-Foreclosure + Probate”)

LIST TYPES TO SUPPORT



Your system must support tagging from:



Pre-Foreclosure

Tax Delinquent

Probate

Code Violations

Water Shut-Off

Vacant

Evictions

Tax Liens

Fire Damage

Divorce

Bankruptcy

Demolition List

Tax Sale List

Notice of Lien

AUTOMATION REQUIREMENTS



Your spreadsheet should automatically:



Detect duplicate addresses

Merge/stack data into one row per property

Add new tags when duplicates are found

Calculate a Motivation Score (based on number of tags)

Rank properties (highest motivation first)

TEST DATA



Create:



At least 2 sample lists (10–15 properties each)

Include overlapping properties



Then demonstrate:



How your system stacks the lists

How tags are added

How properties are ranked

BONUS (STRONGLY PREFERRED)

Conditional formatting (highlight high motivation leads)

Clean layout (easy to use)

System that allows easy import of new lists

HOW TO APPLY (IMPORTANT)



Submit:



Your completed spreadsheet (Google Sheets preferred)

A short explanation (text or video) explaining how your system works



Only applicants who submit this will be considered.



IMPORTANT NOTE



This role will involve:



Cleaning raw data

Converting files (PDF ? Excel)

Stacking large datasets

Preparing data for skip tracing



You must be able to handle large datasets (tens to hundreds of thousands of records), or at minimum be able to process data efficiently in batches without breaking the system.



HOW TO SUBMIT YOUR WORK



Submit your work directly in your application:



A Google Sheets link to your system

(Set access to “Anyone with the link can view”)

A short explanation (text or video)



Do NOT ask for an
. Do NOT send files. Only Google Sheets links will be reviewed.



If your link does not work or requires access, your application will be ignored.



Put the word “STACKER” at the top of your application.



NEXT STEP (READ THIS)



If your syste
ets the requirements, you will be selected for a short live test.



During this test, you will be given real data and asked to:



Import and clean the data

Stack it into the system

Tag and organize properties correctly



If you perform well, you will be hired immediately and begin working on real datasets.

title: Content Quality Analyst
description:
We’re hiring a Content Quality Analyst to help ensure the quality and consistency of web content across our firm’s website. This junior-level role is well-suited for someone who’s detail-oriented, organized, and comfortable working within legal subject matters. You should be confident reviewing content with a critical eye, open to learning, and willing to speak up when you spot issues or areas for improvement. You’ll also work with other teams to keep everything consistent and up to standard across the site. A legal background isn’t required, but the ability to understand legal concepts and apply established guidelines accurately is essential.



Key Responsibilities

Review web content for legal marketing compliance, factual and conceptual accuracy, grammar, and readability.

Conduct regular audits of web pages for both content and technical issues.

Ensure SEO best practices are implemented throughout all content (keywords, meta tags, headers, etc.).

Perform basic technical checks (e.g., internal links, alt text, metadata, formatting).

Coordinate with different teams to ensure consistent application of content standards.

Track content updates and maintain documentation of reviews and recommendations.

Support the development of internal content standards and style guides.



Qualifications And Skills

Excellent attention to detail and the ability to identify content inconsistencies or errors.

Strong organizational and time management skills.

Ability to follow complex editorial, legal, and brand standards accurately.

Fast learner with the ability to adapt to legal content and digital platforms.

Strong written communication and analytical skills.

Familiarity with legal terminology, especially in the U.S. legal landscape (preferred).

Understanding of basic website development concepts such as HTML tags and page structure (preferred).



Basic Requirements

Internet Speed: Minimum of 100Mbps with a backup connection in case of outages



Work Details

Location: Anywhere in the Philippines

Stable employment

A-Z benefits, including bonuses and annual raises

Work-life balance 

Work-from-home opportunity

mbers to maintain a virtual office via Google Meet with cameras on during work hours. This helps us simulate an in-office environment, encourages collaboration, and ensures everyone is present and engaged throughout the day, even while working remotely.

Note: The company uses a time tracker to ensure productivity and accountability.

title: Shopify PageFly Designer & Conversion Specialist
description:
Zentra Digital is looking for an A-player Shopify/PageFly Designer.



We build high-converting Shopify stores and landing pages for e-commerce and DTC brands, and we are looking for someone who understands conversion-driven design, not just someone who can drag and drop sections into place. We need someone who thinks in buyer psychology, page flow and nice design with own initiative.



Responsibilities

- Design and build high-converting Shopify pages using PageFly as the primary page builder

- Create landing pages, product pages, collection pages, and homepage layouts optimized for conversions

- Build custom sections, templates, and reusable page components within PageFly

- Collaborate with the media buying and strategy team to align landing pages with ad creatives

- Work with Figma or Canva to design layouts before building them in PageFly



What We Expect From You

- You will primarily work with PageFly to build and customize Shopify pages. You need to know this tool inside and out. Custom sections, liquid snippets, responsive settings, integrations with Shopify's native features, and how to push PageFly beyond its default templates.

- Experience with conversion rate optimization is essential, because we don't just build pages that look nice — we build pages that sell. 



Part of your role will also include reviewing competitor stores, analyzing top-performing landing pages, and proactively suggesting design improvements based on data and best practices. As a small note to confirm applicants actually read the description carefully, please include the words White Worm somewhere in your application message.



Requirements

- Strong experience with PageFly (this is our primary page builder — you must know it well)

- Solid understanding of Shopify and its ecosystem (themes, apps, liquid basics)

- Understanding of conversion rate optimization (CRO) and direct response design principles

- Design skills using, Canva, or similar tools for design and layout planning

- Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with quick turnaround times



Who We Are Looking For? We are looking for A-players only!

- You take ownership of every page you build and treat it like your own store

- You think about performance and conversions, not just aesthetics

- You're detail-oriented — pixel-perfect spacing, consistent styling, and flawless mobile views matter to you

- You 

- You're comfortable working in a fast-moving e-commerce environment where results matter more than opinions

- You want to grow long term with a serious team 





What We Offer?

- Full-time remote position with flexible working hours

- Long-term opportunity with Zentra Digital

- Opportunity to grow in the company 

- Performance-based bonuses and incentives

- Work on high-revenue Shopify stores for scaling e-commerce and DTC brands

- Direct collaboration with experienced media buyers, strategists, and funnel builders



And of course;  a warm, welcoming environment where you genuinely feel like part of the team, not just another contractor on a list



If u are interested, please send:

1. Examples of Shopify pages or landing pages you've built (preferably using PageFly)

2. A short introduction about you, your experience and why you'd be a great fit

3. Your availability and prefer working hours per month





We look forward to hearing from you!



Kind regards,



The Zentra Digital Team

title: Remote Executive Assistant — Operations, Training + Marketing Support
description:
MHPHC REMOTE EXECUTIVE ASSISTANT — JOB POSTING

For: OnlineJobs.ph

My Happy Place Home Care | Anderson, SC USA | Full-Time Remote





JOB TITLE

Remote Executive Assistant — Operations, Training + Marketing Support





ABOUT THE COMPANY

My Happy Place Home Care is a non-medical home care agency based in Anderson, South Carolina. We serve approximately 45 clients across 10 counties in Upstate South Carolina. Our mission is simple: we don't just provide care — we create peace.



We are a growing, systems-driven agency with documented SOPs, locked call handling scripts, a digital loop tracking system, a structured training program, and an active social media presence. We are looking for one sharp, reliable, long-term person who can learn our entire operation and become an extension of our leadership team.





ABOUT THE ROLE

This is not a task-based VA position. This is a long-term executive support role for someone who is highly organized, learns systems quickly, takes initiative, communicates with professionalism, and can handle multiple types of work in a single day.



You will learn our complete operating system — how we handle calls, how we track issues, how we document, how we train staff, and how we show up on social media. Over time you will take on a training role — helping onboard and develop new tea
mbers using our documented training modules. You will also support the leadership team with admin tasks, content creation, social media, audits, and other projects as needed.



The right person will become indispensable. This is a role you grow into — not a role you age out of.





YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES WILL INCLUDE



OPERATIONS SUPPORT

- Learn and execute our complete call handling system — 15 locked scripts, routing protocols, escalation procedures

- Cover phone operations when needed — fill in for Office Coordinator during absences

- Manage the daily loop tracker — open, update, and close issues

- Support Morning Launch and daily EVV review when needed

- Document calls and interactions per our locked standards



TRAINING SUPPORT

- Learn our full training system — Foundation modules and Tier 2 training modules

- Help onboard new employees using our documented training content

- Score calls using our Avoma call scorecard

- Assist with building and updating training materials as the agency grows



MARKETING AND SOCIAL MEDIA

- Create and schedule social media content across
, Instagram, and other platforms

- Write captions and posts aligned to MHPHC brand voice — warm, professional, never salesy

- Assist with recruitment marketing — job posts, caregiver outreach content

- Support community outreach content for referral partners and families



EXECUTIVE ADMIN SUPPORT

- Assist with audits — compliance tracking, document reviews, file checks

- Research tasks as assigned

- Help build and organize documents, SOPs, and training materials

- Calendar management and scheduling support for leadership

- Any other tasks assigned by the Care Specialist





WHAT YOU WILL BE TRAINED ON

- 15 locked call handling scripts and complete call flow

- MHPHC routing table and escalation protocols

- Universal Loop System — digital entry, tracking, and resolution

- AxisCare — scheduling, client profiles, EVV documentation

- Documentation standards — 3-component note structure, 13 approved categories

- MHPHC brand voice and social media standards

- Training module system — how we onboard and develop staff

- Avoma call scoring platform





WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

- Excellent spoken and written English — clear, warm, professional in all communication

- Strong social media skills — content creation, scheduling, platform management

- Experience in customer service, healthcare admin, or executive support preferred

- Highly organized and detail-oriented — you catch errors before they become problems

- Ability to follow structured systems precisely and also think independently when needed

- Tech-savvy — comfortable learning new platforms quickly

- Reliable internet connection and quiet work environment

- Long-term commitment — we invest heavily in training and expect you to grow with us

- Available Monday through Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM EST with flexibility





WHAT WE OFFER

- Full-time remote position

- Competitive monthly salary based on experience

- Comprehensive structured onboarding — you will learn the entire system before working independently

- Weekly feedback and coaching

- Clear path to a senior role as the agency grows toward franchise expansion

- Long-term stable employment with a leadership team that invests in its people

- Opportunity to grow from assistant to trainer to operations lead over time





HOW TO APPLY

To be considered you must complete all three steps. Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.



STEP 1 — Voice Recording (Required)

Send a voice recording of 1 to 2 minutes introducing yourself and reading this sentence exactly as written:

"Thank you for calling My Happy Place Home Care. This is [Your Name]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with?"



STEP 2 — Written Questions (Required)

Answer all four questions:

1. Describe a time you followed a strict process or script at work. What was it and how did you handled it?

2. What does excellent customer service sound like on a phone call?

3. What social media platforms are you most experienced with and what kind of content have you created?

4. What hours are you available EST?



STEP 3 — Include Your Expected Monthly Salary in USD



Applications without the voice recording will not be reviewed.





My Happy Place Home Care

1638 N Main Street, Anderson, SC
"We don't just provide care — we create peace."

, and Instagram (secondary).

, and Instagram

Design content that builds trust, credibility, and engagement within target communities

title: Creator & Community Partnerships Coordinator
description:
About the brand



CATSUMO is a fast-growing DTC cat toy brand based in San Francisco. We make sumo-themed wrestling glove puppets for cats — and our customers love us. We're scaling quickly and building a content engine to match. You'll be joining a small, fast-moving team where your work directly impacts growth.



About the role:



We're looking for a detail-oriented, proactive coordinator to own our UGC and outreach operations. This is a dual-function role covering creator/influencer outreach and community seeding (cat cafes, shelters, pet businesses). You'll be the person who keeps our content pipeline full and our partnerships organized.



What you'll do:



Research and build targeted lists of micro-influencers and UGC creators in the cat/pet niche

Send personalized outreach, follow up consistently, and manage relationships

Coordinate product seeding — briefing creators, tracking shipments, collecting content

Research and outreach to cat cafes and animal shelters for product partnerships

Manage a content tracker to keep deliverables, deadlines, and usage rights organized

Send and track content usage agreements for all seeding partners

Coordinate with our 3PL fulfillment team to ship product to partners



What we're looking for:



Strong written English — your outreach messages represent our brand

Highly organized with strong attention to detail

Fast responder who can iterate quickly and communicate proactively

Experience with influencer outreach, PR seeding, or community management is a plus

Comfortable with Google Sheets, Gmail, and basic research tools

Genuine love of cats is a real bonus

Paid test assignments — $20 USD each

Before we move to interviews, all applicants complete two short paid assignments. These are real tasks that reflect your day-to-day work. Quality, speed, and communication during the assignment are all evaluated.



Assignment 1 — UGC & influencer outreach



$20 USD on completion



Find 20 micro-influencers or UGC creators in the cat/pet niche who would be a strong fit to receive a free CATSUMO product in exchange for content. Then write a ready-to-send outreach message we could use as a template.



Deliverable:



1. Share a Google Sheet with 20 creators — name, handle, platform, follower count, engagement rate, why they're a good fit

2. Draft an outreach message template (DM and
 format)



We'll evaluate on:



Quality of creator selection — do they actually fit the brand?

Accuracy and format of Google Sheet

Outreach message quality and tone -- is it something a real person would respond to?

Turnaround time and communication during the task



Assignment 2 — Cat cafe & animal shelter seeding



$20 USD on completion



Research cat cafes and animal shelters across the US that would be strong candidates to receive complimentary CATSUMO products.



Deliverables:



1. Share a Google Sheet with 20 venues — name, location, contact
 or form link, Instagram handle (if any), follower count, why they're a good fit.

2. Draft an outreach
 template that introduces CATSUMO, offers a free product, and asks for content/photos in return. Requests permission for any footage to be used in paid ads.



We'll evaluate on:



Quality of venue selection — active, social-media-present, cat-focused

Accuracy and completeness of contact information

Accuracy and format of Google sheet

 tone — warm, genuine, and brand-appropriate

Turnaround time and proactive communication



How to apply:



Send a short introduction telling us about yourself and any relevant experience. If you've done influencer outreach, PR seeding, or community coordination before — tell us about it. No long cover letters needed. We value clarity and directness. We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Customer Experience & Operations Associate (E-commerce Coffee Company) *Full Time, Remote | Long-Term Growth Opportunity
description:
Are you ready…

To start a role that motivates and excites you?

And a position where we invest in your learning, education, and growth?



Then keep reading, we want to work with you!





WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS POSITION:

This is not your typical support role — you’ll work directly with our founders, influence company-wide systems, and gain exposure to every part of the business.



Incredible company culture:

• Small, close team with collaborative, friendly and open culture.

• We believe the biggest asset of a company is our people. We focus on helping each tea
mber reach their full potential by investing in their learning, growth, skills, and education. When we grow as individuals, we succeed as a team.

• Not a fan of office politics or drama? We’re on the same page. Instead of wasting time with that, we focus on solving problems and reaching goals

• 100% remote, enjoy the comfort and flexibility of working from home



Pay, benefits, progression, and long-term growth:

• Competitive salary

• Benefits including up to 16 days a year Paid Time off

• Progression as we grow: greater responsibilities, leadership opportunities, and increased pay.

• Feel empowered, make decisions, have an impact

• Grow with the company and live up to your potential!

• Join an exciting, fast-growing company!







ABOUT US:

We’re a young, ambitious, and small (but growing!) company that’s making a massive impact in ecommerce. 



Since 2015 we’ve been obsessed with building award-winning products and awesome brands that people fall in love with. We have 2 primary brands in our portfolio: 

(1) an international award-winning coffee products brand 
 / 
).

(2) an educational coaching program that helps entrepreneurs grow their businesses to create a positive impact in the world!



Our two recent flagship products became the world’s most successful launches in their categories. We’ve shipped more than 200,000 orders worldwide and we’re continuing to expand.



Our culture is transparent and candid. You’re always welcome (and encouraged!) to ask questions or express a difference of opinion. Learning and growth is a cornerstone of our culture, both personally and professionally. We work hard, and we have fun!







WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

We’re looking for a proactive and detail-oriented team player to fill this role—someone who thrives on solving problems, staying organized, and making things run smoothly across departments.



Do you love helping customers, supporting a team, and keeping operations organized? Do you get satisfaction from making systems work better and “getting things done”? If yes, we want you on our team!



You’re probably the type of person who:

• Enjoys supporting others and finding solutions to problems

• Has strong communication and organizational skills

• Likes working behind the scenes to make sure everything runs like clockwork

• Is excited to wear multiple hats and be part of a growing, fast-moving company

• Has worked directly with founders or in a startup, and enjoys that fast-paced, flexible vibe

We’re not just looking for someone who can support our team, we’re looking for someone to be proactive with taking ownership of tasks, solve problems independently, and grow into larger responsibilities over time.







YOUR OPPORTUNITY AND ROLE ON THE TEAM:

Our culture is collaborative, friendly, and open. We hire people who are smart, ambitious, and have a strong desire to learn and grow.



Sound like you? This is an exciting long-term opportunity!



This is a fully remote position. You’ll work directly with our founders and collaborate with our small but mighty team to support customer happiness, streamline operations, and take on projects that help the business grow. 



This is a foundational role — one where you’ll gain visibility into all parts of the company.



As you grow, your role will grow with you, based on your interests and strengths:

• Want to work improving our systems and processes? Awesome!

• Have ideas on how to improve our website? Fantastic!

• Want to learn how to manage projects? Let’s go!



We’re not just hiring for a role — we’re hiring for potential. 



Your responsibilities will span 4 key areas: customer experience (30-40%), operations/logistics (20-30%), executive support (10-20%), and special projects (10-20%) — giving you exposure across the business. These percentages are estimates and you can expect your day-to-day to shift and evolve as the business grows and as you take ownership. 



1. Customer Experience

• Provide
/chat-based support for our customers across our channels (Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale)

• Answer order and product questions, resolve issues promptly, and ensure customers feel heard and supported

• Support wholesale partners with order updates, invoicing, and coordination



2. Order Management & Platform Coordination

• Manage, monitor, and troubleshoot orders across Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale channels

• Troubleshoot and resolve shipping or fulfillment issues with partners and customers

• Maintain accurate product listings, inventory data, and logistics information across platforms



3. Executive & Admin Support

• Manage founder and team inboxes, schedule meetings, and organize internal documentation

• Assist with travel bookings, meeting preparation, and note-taking

• Support small projects by tracking timelines, managing tasks, and ensuring follow-through



4. Special Projects & Company Operations

• Conduct research projects to support decision-making (tools, systems, competitors)

• Support lead generation efforts through identifying new wholesale prospects, brand partners, or influencers

title: Part-Time Virtual Video Designer
description:
We are seeking a Part-Time Virtual Video Designer to work remotely, creating visually stunning video content that explores the beauty of various countries while showcasing YOOFU’s cultural exchange events and curriculum. As a Video Designer, you’ll play a key role in bringing our mission to life. Flexibility in working hours and a collaborative team spirit are essential, as you’ll be working with an international team across different time zones.





Key Responsibilities:

*Design and produce video content that highlights the beauty of different countries and cultures, as well as YOOFU’s events and curriculum.

*Create motion graphics, animations, and other visual elements to enhance storytelling.

*Design posters for various social media platforms to promote our events and initiatives in collaboration with embassies from different countries.

*Collaborate closely with remote tea
mbers, including video editors, content creators, and project managers, ensuring effective communication and teamwork.

*Ensure that all video designs and posters align with YOOFU’s brand and global mission.

*Maintain flexibility to accommodate different time zones and project deadlines while working with a distributed team.

*Stay current with video design trends and tools to keep YOOFU’s content fresh and engaging.



Requirements:

*Proven experience as a Video Designer with a strong portfolio of motion graphics, animations, and video content.

*Proficiency in tools like Adobe After Effects, Premiere Pro, or similar software.

*Strong visual storytelling skills, with an eye for international culture and aesthetics.

*Flexibility in working hours to meet deadlines and collaborate across time zones.

*Strong team collaboration and communication skills, essential for remote work.



Preferred:

*Experience in international or cultural exchange projects.

*Knowledge of Chinese and global cultural elements.



Work Environment:

This is a part-time, fully remote position. Flexibility and strong communication are required to collaborate effectively with our international team. You will manage your hours, ensuring you meet project deadlines and contribute actively to team discussions.



How to Apply:

Submit your resume, portfolio, and a brief cover letter to this platform. We’re excited to see how your creativity can help showcase the beauty of global cultural exchanges!

title: Virtual Sales Assistant — Property Management Outreach
description:
About Us:

On The Fly Waste Solutions is a fast-growing valet trash and waste management company based in Central Florida. We serve resort communities, HOAs, and multifamily properties across the region. We are expanding rapidly and looking for a driven, professional virtual assistant to help us manage inbound leads, follow up with prospects, and support our sales and outreach efforts.



About the Role:

You will be the first point of contact for new inquiries that come through our website and
. You will also be responsible for proactive outreach to property management companies and real estate developers on our behalf. This is a highly important role — you represent our brand and your professionalism directly impacts our ability to win new contracts.



Responsibilities:



Respond to all inbound website form submissions and
 inquiries within 1 hour using provided
 templates

Follow up with prospects who have not responded to proposals or previous
s

Conduct outbound
 outreach to property managers and real estate developers using provided scripts and templates

Research property management companies and development companies to identify procurement contacts and decision makers

Fill out vendor partnership and supplier applications on company websites

Update and maintain our CRM (HubSpot) with accurate lead status, notes, and contact information

Schedule site visits, walkthroughs, and calls on behalf of the CEO

Compile weekly lead tracking reports summarizing outreach activity and responses

Assist with general administrative tasks as needed





Requirements:



Excellent written English — professional, clear, and natural sounding

Strong verbal English communication skills for occasional phone or video calls

Previous experience in customer service, sales support, or virtual assistant roles

Experience with CRM tools such as HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar platforms

Proficient in Gmail, Google Sheets, and Google Docs

Highly organized, self-motivated, and able to work independently with minimal supervision

Reliable internet connection and quiet working environment

Available to work Eastern Time business hours





Preferred but Not Required:



Experience working with US-based real estate, property management, or construction companies

Familiarity with LinkedIn outreach and lead research

Experience with VoIP phone systems such as OpenPhone or Google Voice





What We Provide:



Detailed
 templates, scripts, and training materials

Access to HubSpot CRM and company

US-based phone number via VoIP

Direct communication with the CEO for guidance and feedback

Opportunity to grow with a rapidly expanding company

title: Direct Response Copywriter (Short-Form Video Ads – Meta)
description:
I’m hiring a direct response copywriter for short-form video ads on
 and Instagram.



- This is NOT a UGC role (no acting)

- This is NOT a static ad role (no graphic design)

- This is NOT e-mail marketing

- This is not a social media manager role



This is a results-driven ad writing role focused on Meta 
/Instagram) conversions.

___

Your job is simple: write hooks that make cat owners:

- Stop scrolling

- Feel something

- Rethink how they’re treating their cat… 

- Then connect into a proven ad body already created for you.



You’ll be writing short-form VSL-style scripts (30–90 seconds), but you only need to create the hooks and angles. The main body is already done for you.

___

I’m looking for someone who can write lines like:

“You’re a
 hurting your cat everyday, and you don’t even realize it.”

“I’ve had 4 cats in my life… and I’m embarrassed to say I just learned this.”

"There’s a 
nt every cat parent eventually faces…"



Not:

“Does your cat scratch the couch?”

“Is your cat bored?”

"Is your cat lonely?"



ChatGPT can do this already.

___

The MOST important part of writing a script is creating a good first line (pattern interrupter) to make people stop scrolling,

then introducing an angle/problem in a storytelling fashion, to get people interested to keep listening. 

We want people to feel like they are watching organic content. 

***If you can't get them to stop scrolling, the rest of the script is useless.

___



You need to understand:

- Scroll-stopping pattern interrupters

- Fast, natural, storytelling flow (no boring parts)

- Selling without sounding like an ad

- Writing for video (short lines, voiceover pacing, B-roll flow)

___



Daily work is simple:

- Think of a ad set angle

- write 3 hook variations per day

- About 1-2 hours per day, depending on your speed

- Monday–Friday.

___



Pay is 10,000 PHP/month for a 30-day trial for part time work. If you create a profitable ad, this can increase to 20k–30k PHP/month, or SUBSTANTIALLY more depending on output. 

***Bottom line, if you can create ads that generate profit, you can make your price. 

___



Hiring Process:

1) Send me a message here, showing that you understand how this job works.

***I'm going to get a lot of applications, so if you can't get me interested in your application right away, you won't be able to stop people from scrolling. 



2) If we feel you have potential, I’ll send you a few top-performing ad examples and you’ll write 3 hooks using the same structure. I’ll send 300 PHP upfront before you start for your practice ad. (I only pay on 
) 



3) If you do that well, I'll send you 2,500php  for 5 days of work, for 1 ad set per day (one angle with 3 hook variations)



4) If you submit good work, have good communication, and good work ethic, we will do 3 more weeks.



5) If you do well for a month, we can discuss increasing salary and output. 



6) After 3 months, you will start earning commission based on ad performance.

That’s where you come in.

Ensure clients always feel informed, supported, and confident throughout the process



Who You Are

Strong communicator — professional, clear, and concise in English

Tech-savvy — comfortable with CRMs (GoHighLevel), Google Drive, and Slack

Send your:

title: Advanced Dispute Specialist (SALARY +BONUSES)
description:
Who We Are

Altura Funding is a business funding and capital solutions company based in Cleveland, Ohio. We move fast and we expect results. We are bringing our credit repair operations fully in-house and we need someone who already knows what they are doing — not someone we have to train from scratch.



What This Role Is

You will manage consumer credit dispute campaigns on behalf of our clients using legally compliant, aggressive dispute strategies under the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA). You will challenge inaccurate, unverifiable, and outdated negative items across all three credit bureaus. This is not a basic dispute role. We expect sophisticated, consumer-law-based strategies executed at high volume with fast turnaround.



What You Will Do



Draft and submit customized, consumer-law-based dispute letters to Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion



Challenge negative items using FCRA Section 611 (accuracy and completeness), Section 605 (obsolete items), and Section 623 (furnisher responsibilities)



Use FDCPA-based debt validation letters to challenge collection accounts



Write goodwill adjustment requests, pay-for-delete proposals, and method of verification demands



Track all dispute timelines, bureau responses, and client file updates with meticulous accuracy



Coordinate with our team on client readiness for business funding as credit profiles improve



Manage multiple client files simultaneously without missing deadlines



Communicate client progress clearly and on schedule



Who You Are



You are not someone who logs into Credit Repair Cloud, DisputeBee, or any other platform and blasts out generic templates. We have seen what that produces and it is not good enough for our clients. We need someone who understands the law, knows how to craft custom dispute arguments, and can move the needle fast.



You know that real results come from understanding why a negative item is on the report, identifying the correct legal angle to challenge it, and executing with precision. You do not guess. You do not wait. You know that bureaus have 30 days to respond and you use every tool available within that window to get results.



If you need six months to start producing, this is not the role for you. Our clients need movement quickly and we expect to see results within the first 30 days of working a file. If you have a proven track record and documented results to show for it, we want to hear from you. If you do not, please do not apply.



What You Must Have



Minimum 2 years of hands-on credit dispute experience — not just using software, actually writing and managing disputes from strategy to resolution



Deep working knowledge of FCRA, FDCPA, and Metro 2 compliance standards



Ability to draft original, legally grounded dispute letters — not template blasts



Experience working directly with all three major bureaus and their dispute processes



Familiarity with credit repair platforms but the ability to operate beyond their built-in templates



Extreme attention to detail and the ability to manage high volume without errors



Strong written English communication skills



Documented results you can show during the interview — we will ask



What We Do Not Do

We operate strictly within the bounds of federal consumer protection law. We do not promise specific score increases, we do not dispute accurate information, and we do not use deceptive or fraudulent practices. If that is not how you operate, this is not the right fit.



Compensation

Competitive base pay based on experience. Performance bonuses tied to documented client results. Long-term opportunity for the right person.



To Apply

Send your resume, a description of your credit repair experience, and at least two documented examples of dispute outcomes you have achieved for clients. Applications without proven results will not be reviewed.

title: Product Designer – Longshot Labs
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a Product Designer to be the first dedicated design hire at Longshot, a prediction market focused on deep liquidity and user experience. You will work directly with the founders to build the product experience from the ground up, owning everything from initial ideation to production-ready specs.

What You'll Do

Own the core product experience 

You'll be the design voice for Longshot's most critical surfaces, including the trading and markets pages where users place predictions, and the onboarding flows that turn new visitors into active traders. You'll think in real interactions: order states, loading behaviors, edge cases, empty states, and the small 
nts that make a product feel trustworthy rather than thrown together.

Build and evolve the design system 

You will take ownership of a design system that has some initial components, but is still in development. You will walk through what already exists, establish component standards, define tokens, and build a library that scales as the product grows. Your system will be the foundation that lets the rest of the team ship faster without losing consistency.

Drive ideation to handoff 

You will be in the room from the earliest conversations through to engineering handoff. That means contributing to requirements discussions, iterating quickly on concepts, producing clean Figma files with clear components and annotations, and providing clarifications as engineers perform wiring.

Think like an owner 

You'll have bi-weekly design reviews directly with the founders and a seat at the table on product decisions. We want someone who voices their opinions when something doesn't serve the user and measures success by the look of the final product.

Drive decisions related to graphic, branding, and marketing assets

Longshot's visual identity doesn't stop at the app. You'll own how we look everywhere else too: the marketing site, brand assets, graphics, all of it. Same care, same eye, same standards as the product. When something looks off, you'll catch it. And you'll fix it.

What We're Looking For

You have 1 to 4 years of experience as a product or UX designer, ideally at a startup. You're deeply comfortable in Figma. You use AI tools to naturally complement your workflow, but major decisions are still driven by your thinking. You have strong interaction design instincts and think in systems and flows, not just individual screens. You are intrinsically motivated and thrive in a remote environment.

Bonus points if you have

Prior exposure to crypto, consumer-facing applications, and/or gaming interfaces

Experience building or contributing to a design system early on

Some background in user research or usability testing

An eye for motion and microinteractions that make dense UIs feel alive

What success looks like

In your first few months, you'll have continued to maintain the design system, owned end-to-end design for at least two major product surfaces, and built a design rhythm with the founding team that keeps quality high as we move quickly. Over time, you'll become the design standard-bearer for Longshot, and as the team grows, you'll help define what great design looks like.

What We Offer

Meaningful ownership with significant equity as the first design hire

Direct access to founders with a real voice in product direction from day one

A fully remote, high-trust environment where you own your work

Real impact: your designs ship to real users quickly



 me at 
 with your portfolio if interested

title: Tiktok Shop  Manager
description:
About Inspiratek

Inspiratek is a U.S.-based e-commerce company generating $16M+ in annual revenue across Amazon, TikTok Shop, and direct-to-consumer channels. We own and operate a portfolio of 5 premium bedding brands sold primarily through Amazon US, with a rapidly growing TikTok Shop channel currently generating ~$2.5M annually.

We are a data-driven, operationally disciplined team of 27+ people. Every decision we make is backed by metrics, every initiative has a clear owner and kill criteria, and every tea
mber is expected to operate with autonomy and accountability.



We are not a startup experimenting with TikTok. We are a profitable, scaling business that needs a proven operator to own and grow our TikTok Shop channel from $2.5M to $5M+ within 12 months.



The Role

You will be the single owner of TikTok Shop operations across our entire brand portfolio. This is not a content creation role or a virtual assistant position — this is a channel management role with full P&L responsibility. You will manage the affiliate/creator ecosystem, oversee GMV Max ad campaigns, coordinate product launches on TikTok, and drive profitable revenue growth.

You report directly to the CEO. You will work cross-functionally with our Amazon operations team, creative/product development team, logistics team, and external agencies. You will be evaluated on revenue growth, profitability, creator activation rates, and content velocity — not activity metrics.



What You Will Own

1. TikTok Shop Strategy & Revenue Growth

• Develop and execute the TikTok Shop strategy across all brands (currently 5 bedding brands)

• Own the channel P&L — you are responsible for GMV, ROAS, net margin, and CAC

• Set monthly/quarterly revenue targets and build the execution plan to hit them

• Identify and capitalize on TikTok trends, seasonal opportunities, and promotional events (e.g., Super Brand Day, Mega Sales)

• Manage product catalog on TikTok Seller Center — listings, pricing, inventory coordination, promotions

• Coordinate with Amazon ops to prevent cross-channel cannibalization and align launch timing

2. Creator & Affiliate Program Management

• Build and scale a high-performing creator network (target: 200+ active creators within 6 months)

• Recruit creators through Affiliate Center, manual outreach, and community building

• Develop tiered commission structures that reward performance without destroying margin

• Manage product sampling/seeding program — budget allocation, creator selection criteria, fulfillment coordination

• Track creator activation rates (% of seeded creators who actually post) and implement re-engagement sequences

• Build a creator community (Slack/
) with content calendars, best practices, and incentive programs

• Coach creators on content optimization: hooks, CTAs, product demonstration, FTC compliance

3. GMV Max & Paid Advertising

• Manage GMV Max campaigns with budgets of $500–$5,000+/day across multiple brands

• Structure campaigns by product, objective, and creative type

• Optimize for ROAS, not just GMV — every dollar spent must be justified by margin contribution

• Identify and amplify top-performing organic creator content via Spark Ads

• A/B test creative angles, hooks, and CTAs at scale

• Know when to scale, when to pause, and when to kill — with data to support every decision

4. Performance Analytics & Reporting

• Build and maintain weekly performance reports covering: GMV, orders, AOV, ROAS, CAC, creator posting frequency, content velocity, affiliate commission costs, net margin

• Identify trends before they become problems — declining CVR, creator churn, rising CPA, content velocity drops

• Calculate true profitability per product on TikTok (revenue minus COGS, commissions, ad spend, sampling costs, fulfillment, returns)

• Present data-driven recommendations to leadership with clear trade-offs and financial impact

5. Cross-Functional Coordination

• Align TikTok launches with Amazon product launches and inventory planning

• Work with creative team on product photography, video assets, and listing content optimized for TikTok

• Coordinate with logistics on TikTok-specific fulfillment (FBT, seller shipping, sample shipments)

• Manage relationships with external agencies or freelancers supporting TikTok operations



Requirements

Must Have (Non-Negotiable)

• 2–4+ years of direct TikTok Shop management experience as a seller (not just as a creator or agency)

• Proven track record of managing $50K+/month in TikTok Shop GMV with documented results

• Hands-on experience with TikTok Seller Center, Affiliate Center, and GMV Max campaign management

• Demonstrated ability to recruit, manage, and scale a creator/affiliate network of 50+ active creators

• Strong analytical skills — comfortable building reports, calculating margins, and making data-driven decisions

• Experience managing GMV Max campaigns with budgets of $500+/day and achieving ROAS > 3.0x

• Financial literacy — understand unit economics, contribution margin, break-even ROAS

• Native or near-native English proficiency

Strong Preference

• Experience in home goods, bedding, lifestyle, or wellness categories on TikTok Shop

• Experience managing multiple brands on TikTok simultaneously

• Familiarity with TikTok Shop analytics tools (Kalodata, FastMoss, or similar)

• Experience with product seeding/sampling programs at scale (100+ samples/month)

• Understanding of Amazon operations (helpful for cross-channel coordination)

• Experience with Live Shopping on TikTok

Nice to Have

• Experience with TikTok Shop API integrations

• Content creation skills (CapCut, video editing)

• Experience scaling a TikTok Shop channel from

title: Senior Video Editor (US-Based Brand | PST Schedule)
description:
Application Requirements:

1. Use at least 10AP for your application.

2. Include "Senior Video Editor Pro" in your subject line.



Note: Applications missing either requirement will not be considered.

_________________________________________________________



About Veritas Vans

Veritas Vans builds the most trusted accessories for Starlink Mini and Starlink Standard. Our mission is to give people the freedom to work, explore, and stay connected anywhere. From Baja coastlines to deep forest trails, our customers rely on our gear for capability, protection, and unlimited connection.



Your role is to help visually tell this story across all platforms.



Role Overview

We are hiring a Senior Video Editor who can own our long-form YouTube content, high-retention short-form content, and graphic design needs. You must be highly experienced, creative, fast, and able to work autonomously within a growing brand.



Responsibilities



Video Editing – Long Form

- Edit 6–15 minute YouTube videos with strong retention, pacing, and narrative clarity.

- Add b-roll, overlays, captions, music, and brand-consistent graphics.

- Build engaging openings with proven YouTube hook methods.



Video Editing – Short Form

- Execute the Hook ? Retain ? Reward method for Reels, TikTok, and Shorts.

- Add dynamic subtitles, zooms, cuts, 
 inserts, and pacing adjustments.

- Produce scroll-stopping, high-retention content.



Graphic Design & Photo Editing

- Create YouTube thumbnails and graphic assets.

- Design text treatments, overlays, and promotional visuals.

- Edit lifestyle and product photos for multi-platform use.



Brand Storytelling

- Align all edits with our brand themes: freedom, connection, capability, control.

- Contribute creative ideas and concepts for future video content.



AI-Driven Editing & Workflow Optimization

- Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Adobe AI, Topaz, Descript, etc.) as a core part of your editing workflow.

- Generate high-performing hooks, captions, and on-screen text callouts using AI.

- Use AI to research and improve storytelling, pacing, and content structure.

- Continuously explore and implement new AI workflows to improve output quality, speed, and efficiency.

- Leverage AI to enhance both creative output and production throughput, not just assist basic tasks.



Cross-Platform Optimization

- Edit in multiple aspect ratios: 16:9, 9:16, 4:5, 1:1.

- Prepare final deliverables in organized, structured folders.

- Optimize content for YouTube, Instagram, TikTok,
.



Collaboration

- Work closely with the content team and leadership.

- Follow SOPs and meet deadlines consistently.

- Join scheduled calls proactively (no reminders required)

- Be punctual and prepared for feedback sessions



Work Discipline & Delivery Standards

- Be actively available and responsive during working hours (PST schedule).

- Address tasks, revisions, and team communication within working hours.

- Meet deadlines consistently and communicate early if delays occur.

- Submit deliverables early in the workday whenever possible to allow time for review and revisions.



Quality & Output Standards

- Deliver edits that are near publish-ready on first submission.

- Ensure drafts require minimal revisions and are not incomplete or partially executed.

- Perform a full self-review before submission to ensure:

- all feedback is applied

- no new issues are introduced

- output meets brand and quality standards

- Maintain consistently high production quality across all deliverables.



Note:

Include the phrase “Senior Video Editor Pro” in your subject line. Applications missing this phrase will not be considered. Use at least 5AP for your application.



Required Skills



- Ability to work graveyard shift (PST hours)

- 3–5+ years professional video editing experience

- Expert in Adobe Premiere Pro

- Strong skills in After Effects, Photoshop, Illustrator, or Figma

- Proven experience editing YouTube content and high-performing short-form videos

- Strong understanding of retention, pacing, story structure, and attention engineering

- Experience creating high-converting thumbnails

- Strong understanding of modern content trends across YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok

- Ability to edit lifestyle, outdoor, product, and UGC-style content



AI & Workflow Skills (Required)

- Strong proficiency in using AI tools (ChatGPT, Adobe AI, etc.) within editing workflows

- Ability to generate hooks, captions, and on-screen text using AI

- Experience using AI to improve storytelling, pacing, and video performance

- Ability to research and implement new AI workflows to improve efficiency and output quality



Work Ethic & Discipline

- Highly reliable, responsive, and accountable

- Strong attention to detail and ability to self-review work

- Ability to follow feedback precisely and minimize repeated revisions



Bonus Experience

- Motion graphics or animation skills.

- Experience in e-commerce, outdoor brands, or creator channels.

- Understanding of YouTube SEO (titles, thumbnails, keywords).

- Ability to generate ideas for hooks, storylines, and visual treatments.



What We Offer

- Long-term employment with growth potential.

- Competitive salary based on experience.

- Consistent weekly workload.

- Direct impact on a fast-scaling global brand.

- Creative freedom and an opportunity to lead our video direction.



How to Apply



Submit the following:

- Resume

- Short explanation of why you’re the right fit

- Brief explanation of how you use AI in your editing workflow

- 1 example where you improved video performance (retention, CTR, etc.)

- Portfolio of your best long-form and short-form edits

- Links to YouTube channels or brands you’ve edited for



Learn About Us:

title: Cold Caller
description:
Job Title: Expert Cold Caller & Sales Closer (Fluent English Required)



Position Type: Remote

Compensation: Base + High Commission (Performance-Based)



About the Role



We are looking for highly skilled **cold callers and sales closers** who are confident in English communication and have real experience in booking appointments and closing deals over calls and Zoom.



This is not a beginner role — we need someone who can **handle objections, build trust quickly, and close deals confidently.



#Your Responsibilities



* Make outbound cold calls using our AI-assisted scripts

* Engage prospects and build instant rapport

* Qualify leads and book appointments at the client’s convenience

* Conduct or assist in Zoom sales calls

* Clearly explain our services and value proposition

* Collect payment details live on Zoom calls

* Close deals ranging from $100 to $500 packages

* Follow up with leads to maximize conversions



#Ideal Candidate Profile



* Proven experience in cold calling & sales closing

* Advanced / fluent English speaking skills (US/International clients)

* Strong confidence and persuasion ability

* Experience with appointment setting + closing on calls

* Comfortable handling objections and rejections

* Ability to work independently and hit targets

* Familiar with Zoom, CRM tools, and sales workflows



#What We Provide: 

* Proven AI-based calling scripts

* Training on our services and closing process

* Qualified lead lists

* Support for improving your closing rate

* High earning potential through commissions



#Compensation Structure



* Base pay (optional depending on experience)

* High commissions per closed deal

* Performance bonuses for consistent closers



#Hiring Process



1. English communication test

2. Live mock cold call / roleplay

3. Short trial task

4. Final onboarding



#Important



We are looking for closers, not just callers.

If you can confidently turn conversations into revenue, this role is for you.



To Apply:

Send a short voice recording introducing yourself + your past sales experience.

title: Sales Development Representative
description:
Sales Representative - Church App Platform

Who We Are

Vinebranch Labs is a nonprofit on a mission to help churches stay connected with their people beyond Sunday.

We are building a platform that gives every church their own app. Not a generic church management tool. A real, branded app for their church that keeps their congregation engaged throughout the entire week.

The centerpiece is something no one else has built: a personalization engine that takes a pastor's weekly sermon and turns it into daily devotionals, Bible study prompts, and scripture readings for the whole congregation, automatically, every week. The pastor preaches on Sunday, and their message reaches their people every single day after that, without the pastor doing any extra work.

On top of that, the app includes a prayer wall where members can share requests and pray for each other, event listings, community messaging, giving, small group tools, and more. Everything a church needs to stay connected in one place.

Most churches today have nothing. Maybe a
 group and a website from 2015. Their people walk out on Sunday and don't engage with the church again until next week. We are changing that.

Our founder previously built a SaaS company with 70+ employees and over $6M in annual revenue. We know how to build and scale software businesses. 

But this time, we are doing it as a nonprofit, with all profits going toward spreading God's Word and supporting Christian causes around the world. 

This is a ministry, not just a tech company.

Why This Matters

Think about what churches are dealing with right now.

Pastors spend hours every week preparing a sermon. They pour their heart into it. They deliver it on Sunday. And then on Monday, most of their congregation has already moved on. The message fades. People drift. By the time next Sunday comes around, they have to start from scratch.

What if that sermon could keep working all week? 

What if every member got a daily devotional on their phone, tied directly to what their pastor taught on Sunday? 

What if the church had a place where people could pray for each other, sign up for events, connect with their small group, and give, all in one app with their church's name on it?

That is what we are building. And we are going to bring it to thousands of churches.

We are starting with Churches in the US and Australia, where most churches have no app at all. The competition is thin, the need is real, and the price point is low so we plan to make it an easy decision for any pastor. We take their sermon, show them what their app could look like, and let the product sell itself.

This is showing a pastor something that makes them say, "I need this for my church."

The Role

We are hiring sales representatives to help us bring this product to churches, starting in the US or Australia.

Your job is simple: reach out to pastors and church leaders, introduce the platform, show them a personalized preview of what their church's app would look like, and help them get started.

What you will do day to day:

Research and build lists of churches to contact

Reach out to pastors and church leaders via
 and phone

Walk them through a demo of the platform and show them what their church's app would look like

Follow up with interested churches and guide them through to signing up

Track your outreach and pipeline activity in a CRM

Share feedback from conversations with pastors so we can improve the product

Build relationships with pastors who can refer other churches to us

What we are looking for:

B2B sales experience, ideally in SaaS or outbound sales (ideally 2 years+ sales experience)

Strong English communication skills (you will be speaking with pastors daily)

A practicing Christian who attends church regularly and genuinely understands church life

Self-motivated and comfortable working independently in a remote role

Comfortable with cold outreach 
 and phone)

Honest, warm, and able to build trust quickly

Why this role is different:

This is not selling software to a corporation that does not care. Every deal you close means a real church community gets a tool that keeps them connected to God's Word throughout the week. You are directly helping pastors extend their ministry. You will hear pastors get excited when they see what their app could look like. That is the daily experience of this job.

You are also getting in on the ground floor. This is an early-stage company with a founder who has already built and scaled a business before. If you are the type of person who wants to be part of building something meaningful from the beginning, not just filling a seat at a big company, this is your opportunity.

Compensation:

Base salary plus commission on every church you close. 

We care about results, not when you are sitting at a desk.



Your Application

Thank you for taking the time to apply. We want to get to know you beyond a resume, which is why we are asking you to answer a few short audio questions.

Please be yourself. We are not looking for perfect answers. We are looking for people who are genuine, who care about this mission, and who can communicate warmly and clearly, because that is exactly what this job requires every day.

God Bless, and we look forward to hearing from you.

Lin k to audio interview

v / apply / founding -sales -rep

Please click the link and submit the audio.

Thank you!

title: Logistics & Supply Chain Lead
description:
COMPANY SNAPSHOT

We’re a growing e-commerce brand focused on bedding and textile products, selling primarily through Amazon. Our catalog spans approximately 20 product lines with deep variation across colors and sizes. We’re in a high-growth phase with strong product-market fit, and we’re building the team to scale significantly over the next 12 months.



WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

Our logistics department has more work than bandwidth. Recurring operations, analytical projects, and new process development all compete for the same limited hours, and two of our four strategic pillars—Supply Chain & Supplier Management and 3PL & FBA Operations—have minimal formal coverage today.

Without this hire, significant financial exposure in storage fees and surcharges continues to compound, supplier negotiation and freight optimization remain unaddressed, and the forecasting process can’t mature into a cross-functional system. This role exists to take ownership of processes and planning so the department can operate across all four pillars simultaneously—and so the financial leaks stop growing.



 WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Inventory Intelligence & Demand Planning — Own days-of-supply targets, safety stock methodology, and the forecasting process across all SKU tiers. Keep hero SKUs within target supply windows. Build a cross-functional forecasting process that pulls from PPC, deals, and channel data to continuously improve accuracy.

Supply Chain & Supplier Management — Build this function from the ground up. Establish the PO lifecycle from issuance to receipt, create supplier scorecards tracking lead time, defect rates, and cost per unit, coordinate QC, and manage freight. Define what good looks like and build toward it.

3PL & FBA Operations — Manage 3PL relationships and FBA inbound logistics. Define and track 3PL SLAs, plan inbound shipments to avoid surcharge threshold breaches, build a reimbursement recovery process, and monitor FC check-in and receiving times.

Cost Optimization & Storage Governance — Drive systematic fee mitigation across long-term storage fees, storage utilization surcharges, shipping rates, and landed cost. Own the financial exposure dashboard. Make sure we never get surprised by a cost cliff we should have seen coming.

Team Development & Process Formalization — Document SOPs for all recurring processes, establish cadences that run without prompting, cross-train to eliminate single points of failure, and develop the team as it grows. Build a department that works even when you’re not in the room.



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In the first 90 days:

You’re independently running the weekly KPI cadence and department check-in

You’ve taken ownership of the Supply Chain and 3PL pillars, with current-state processes documented and first improvements underway

Storage fee exposure and surcharge risk are under active, systematic management—not reactive firefighting

By 12 months:

All four strategic pillars are staffed and operating with documented SOPs

KPIs are trending positively across days of supply, storage fees, surcharges, forecast accuracy, and landed cost

The department can absorb demand fluctuations without crisis-mode escalation, and leadership oversight has shifted from weekly hands-on involvement to monthly strategic reviews



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Requirements:

Strong proficiency in written and spoken English

4–6 years in logistics or supply chain roles, with at least 2 years owning outcomes (not just executing tasks) in a leadership or senior IC capacity

Hands-on Amazon FBA experience—you understand long-term storage fee mechanics, storage utilization surcharges, inbound shipment planning, and fee structures at a tactical level

Experience managing at least one 3PL relationship end-to-end (contracts, SLAs, cost benchmarking, receiving coordination)

Proven track record of building processes from scratch in a resource-constrained environment—not just optimizing what someone else built

Experience managing or building a small logistics team—you’ll be leading existing operators and growing the function over time

Advanced Excel / Google Sheets fluency—this is a spreadsheet-heavy environment and you’ll be building models, dashboards, and trackers from day one

Financial acuity—you naturally translate logistics decisions into dollar impact and use that to drive prioritization

You’ll thrive here if you:

Treat the department’s financial exposure like it’s your own money—you don’t wait to be told something is a problem

Instinctively create repeatable processes rather than solving the same problem manually each time

Can look at a list of urgent items and pick the ones that actually matter this week, then communicate clearly about what’s being deferred and why



THIS ISN’T FOR YOU IF…

Your strength is strategy and frameworks but you’re not excited about personally pulling data and building spreadsheets—at least until the team grows

You need heavy top-down direction to know what to work on, or you need perfect information before you’re willing to make a call

Your experience is entirely in enterprise or Fortune 500 logistics without e-commerce or Amazon exposure

You see a small, early-stage team and think “that’s beneath me” rather than “that’s a chance to build something”





8. WHAT WE OFFER

Work Arrangement

Fully Remote. Company operates on EST hours with flexible scheduling.

Growth Opportunity

This is a ground-floor leadership role. You’ll build the logistics function and grow the team beneath you. As the operation scales, so does your scope and impact.

Team & Culture

You’ll join a lean, high-autonomy team where results matter more than process theater. The CEO provides strategic direction and removes structural blockers; you run the operation. Expect direct communication, low ego, and a bias toward action.



HOW TO APPLY

To apply, submit your resume along with a brief response (3–5 sentences) to the following question:

Describe a time you inherited a logistics or supply chain function with no documented processes and built the operating system from scratch. What did you prioritize first, and why?

This question is your first filter. Candidates who can answer it with specifics—not generalities—are the ones we want to talk to.

title: Cold Callers
description:

Schedule property viewings, consultations, and meetings with our real estate agents

Accurately log all calls, lead information, and outcomes in our CRM system

Follow up with previous leads to nurture relationships and maintain 
ntum

Handle objections professionally and confidently

Maintain a positive attitude and high energy level throughout the day



Requirements



Excellent command of spoken and written English (fluent, clear, professional accent and grammar)

Previous experience in cold calling, telemarketing, sales, or customer service is preferred

Strong communication and persuasion skills

Ability to handle rejection and stay motivated

Self-disciplined and able to work independently (especially if remote)

Basic computer skills (Google Workspace, CRM software, dialing systems)

Positive, friendly, and professional phone presence

Goal-oriented with a hunter mentality



What We Offer



Competitive base salary + high commission structure (uncapped earning potential)

Performance bonuses and incentives

Flexible working hours (full-time  available)

How to Apply

If you have a confident voice, strong English skills, and enjoy talking to people, we want to hear from you!

title: Senior Marketing Lead
description:
Winnie & Ethel's Downtown Diner in Las Vegas, NV is seeking a Senior Marketing Lead to join our team in a part-time capacity (approximately 20 hours per week, flexible schedule).



We are located at the Huntridge Shopping Center, 1130 E Charleston Blvd #140.



Our ideal candidate is proactive, detail-oriented, and experienced in marketing execution, with a strong ability to manage campaigns, coordinate across teams, and elevate our brand presence.



Starting pay: Based on experience (contractor role) + performance-based bonuses



???? Walk-ins will not be accepted. Please apply using the link below.



? How to Apply



Fill out this application form: 



MARKETING LEAD’S MISSION



To execute and coordinate Winnie & Ethel’s marketing initiatives across digital, in-store, and community channels, ensuring strong brand alignment, consistent communication, and measurable impact on guest engagement and revenue.



RESPONSIBILITIES



Marketing & Campaign Execution



Develop and execute marketing campaigns for events, promotions, and seasonal initiatives

Design in-store collateral (flyers, table tents, posters, etc.)

Execute
 and SMS marketing campaigns

Prepare and submit weekly marketing performance reports



Social Media & Content Coordination



Track and analyze social media performance and analytics

Coordinate with internal and external social media/content teams

Conduct influencer outreach and manage partnerships



Guest Engagement & Brand Presence



Respond to guest reviews (Google, Yelp, OpenTable, etc.)

Respond to guest inquiries via
 and social media

Maintain accurate business information across all platforms



Media & Partnerships



Maintain and grow media contact lists

Coordinate with photographers, vendors, and external partners

Identify and pursue press opportunities, awards, and features



Advertising & Performance

Support and manage Google and Meta ad campaigns

Monitor performance and recommend improvements



KEY SKILLS

Strong written and verbal communication

High attention to detail and brand consistency

Proactive and self-driven mindset

Creativity and visual awareness

Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously

Strong organizational and coordination skills



QUALIFICATIONS

Proven experience in marketing and social media (required)

Restaurant marketing experience preferred

Experience in independent or high-volume restaurant environments preferred

Experience with
 marketing platforms and design tools (advanced level)

Familiarity with Google and Meta Ads (preferred)

Ability to work independently and collaboratively



SCHEDULE

Part-time role

Approximately 20 hours per week

Flexible based on operational needs



BENEFITS

Semi-monthly pay

Direct deposit

Performance-based bonuses

Growth opportunities for high-performing team members



PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS

Ability to work on a computer for extended periods and PST timezone

Ability to attend daily/weekly meetings or events as needed



ABOUT WINNIE & ETHEL’S

Modeled after the iconic, round-the-clock American eatery of the 1940s, Winnie & Ethel’s Downtown Diner draws upon the rich history and legacy of Las Vegas’ Huntridge Theater and neighborhood. Chef Aaron Lee and CEO Mallory Gott named the restaurant after his grandma Winnie and her great-grandma Ethel whose warm, loving kitchens were always open.

title: Senior Media Buyer
description:
We're a digital marketing agency looking for an experienced Media Buyer to take ownership of Google Ads and Meta Ads across our client accounts. This is a role for someone who combines strong strategic thinking with hands-on execution and is confident doing both well.



This is a part-time role (20 hours per week) working NZ business hours, with real potential to grow into a full-time position. If you enjoy being in the accounts every day and want to work somewhere your expertise is genuinely valued, we'd love to hear from you.



What you'll be doing

This role blends strategy with daily execution. You'll be:

- Conducting a thorough daily review of Google Ads and Meta Ads accounts across the portfolio

- Monitoring campaign health across key metrics (ROAS, CPA, CTR, CPM, CPC) and acting on what you find

- Developing and applying campaign strategies that align with each client's goals

- Making bid, budget, and targeting adjustments based on live performance data

- Planning and running structured A/B tests across creatives, audiences, and campaign structures

- Identifying strategic opportunities and making proactive recommendations to improve results

- Setting up and launching new campaigns from brief through to live

- Ensuring tracking, attribution, and reporting are accurate and up to date



What we need from you

We're looking for someone who can demonstrate:

- At least 5 years of hands-on, end-to-end experience managing Google Ads and Meta Ads campaigns

- Strong strategic thinking, the ability to look at an account and know what needs to happen next

- A track record of real campaign results across a range of clients or industries

- The ability to diagnose performance issues quickly and independently

- Experience managing multiple accounts at once while maintaining a high standard across all of them

- Clear written communication skills in English and the confidence to keep the team well informed

- Availability to work NZ business hours reliably for the full 20 hours per week

- High attention to detail across every campaign they manage



Tools and Platforms

You should be confident and experienced with:

- Google Ads (Search, Display, Performance Max, Shopping)

- Meta Ads Manager 
 & Instagram)

- Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Google Tag Manager

- Looker Studio for reporting and dashboards

- Project management tools such as Asana, ClickUp, or Monday.com



This role is a great fit if...

- You have solid experience across both Google Ads and Meta Ads and are confident working across multiple accounts

- You think strategically and enjoy finding ways to improve performance, not just maintain it

- You are self-directed and comfortable working remotely with minimal supervision

- Part-time suits where you are right now and you are open to the role growing over time

- You are available to work during NZ business hours





If you are experienced, autonomous, and ready to own a portfolio of accounts from day one, we would love to hear from you. This role starts part time but grows with the right person.

title: Crochet Pattern Designer (PDF Pattern Creation & Proofreading)
description:
Job Description:

We are looking for an experienced crochet pattern designer to help us improve and recreate existing crochet pattern PDFs. Some of our current patterns require refinement or full redevelopment to ensure they are accurate, high-quality, and easy to follow.



This role is focused on producing clear, professional, and fully functional crochet patterns that accurately result in the finished item shown in reference images.



Responsibilities:



Review and proofread existing crochet pattern PDFs for accuracy and clarity

Identify and correct errors, inconsistencies, and unclear instructions

Recreate or redesign patterns when necessary to ensure they produce the intended final item

Deliver clean, well-structured, and easy-to-follow PDF patterns

Ensure proper formatting, stitch instructions, and logical flow



Requirements:



Proven experience in crochet pattern design (not just graphic design)

Ability to create patterns that can be realistically followed to produce the final product

Strong attention to detail and pattern accuracy

Ability to read and write standard crochet terminology (US terms preferred)

Portfolio of completed crochet patterns (required)



Nice to Have:



Experience selling or publishing crochet patterns (e.g., Etsy, Ravelry, etc.)

Ability to format patterns into clean, professional PDFs



Scope of Work:



Ongoing project-based work

Initial test task may be provided

Multiple patterns may be assigned weekly depending on performance



Instructions to Apply:



Share links or samples of crochet patterns you have created (very important)

Briefly describe your experience in crochet pattern design

Confirm if you can recreate a pattern based on finished item images



We are looking for someone reliable and detail-oriented who can help us ensure our patterns are high quality and customer-ready.

title: Graphic Designer
description:
We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented graphic designer to help create Instagram content for our marketing agency, EntreDupe.

- Occasional static posts using photos/videos we provide



We already have:

- A design template

- Clear content ideas

- References to follow



Your job is to take our content and turn it into clean, professional, high-quality visuals.



Responsibilities:

- Design Instagram carousels (6–10 slides)

- Follow our brand style and template closely

- Place text (soundbites) onto images/videos

- Make minor adjustments based on feedback

- Submit all deliverables by Friday each week



Requirements:

- Strong Canva skills (required)

- Good eye for clean, modern design

- Ability to follow directions and replicate a style

- Basic English (must understand instructions clearly)

- Reliable and consistent



Work Details:

- 5 hours per week

- $2 USD/hour

- Work anytime (flexible schedule)

- Weekly deadline: Friday submissions



Expected Output:

~3–4 posts per week (mix of carousels + static posts)



To Apply, Please include:

- Your portfolio (Canva or design samples)

- 2–3 carousel examples you’ve created

- Your favorite Instagram account for design inspiration
